     adversarial verify -> reconcile. goNoGo = go-with-changes. The orchestrator
     independently re-confirmed the load-bearing engine signatures against the headers
     before the build wave:
       insertMIDIClip(ClipOwner&, const juce::String& name, TimeRange)  @ tracktion_ClipOwner.h:115
       insertWaveClip(ClipOwner&, name, file, ClipPosition, DeleteExistingClips) @ :107
       LaunchHandle PlayState/QueueState, getPlayingStatus():49, getQueuedStatus():52,

       SceneList::ensureNumberOfScenes(int):140, getNumScenes/getScenes, sceneWatcher:154 -->
# Forge SessionView — Clip-Launch Grid Design (Sheet 00, PRIMARY view)

> Build-ready reconciled design. `docs/devlog/session-design.md`.
> Every CONFIRMED adversarial fix is folded in; see "Adversarial review: what changed" at the end.

## 0. Goal & guardrails

SessionView is the new **default** `ViewMode` (`Session | Arrange | Mix`). It renders a tracks×scenes clip-launch grid built on Tracktion's `Scene` / `ClipSlot` / `LaunchHandle`, playable with **mouse + keyboard, no hardware**. The shipped 4OSC / piano-roll / mixer ride *inside* slots and scenes (a slot's clip opens in the existing piano-roll drawer; instruments live on the same track plugin chain).

Hard guardrails (from the brief and proven workflow):
- **File-disjoint build.** Every new file under `src/ui/session/` is owned by exactly one build agent. Agents share only headers.
- **Additive-only seams, with ONE disclosed exception.** Edits to `main.cpp`, `ProjectSession.{h,cpp}`, `CMakeLists.txt` are ORCHESTRATOR-owned and purely additive. **`ControlBar.{h,cpp}` is the single non-additive edit** (the 0=Arrange/1=Mixer convention is renumbered to 0=Session/1=Arrange/2=Mixer). It is orchestrator-owned only and MUST land atomically with its two shell map sites (`main.cpp` onViewMode decode + setViewMode int map) in one commit — no second agent touches ControlBar.
- **Message-thread only** for all model edits. `LaunchHandle` play/queue state is *read* for display via message-readable getters — never call `advance()` (audio-thread only).
- **New engine ops go through `ProjectSession`** additive methods, never raw `te::` calls inside the view.
- **Never cache live engine pointers across the poll/repaint boundary** (see §e, §threading). Pads hold `(trackIndex, sceneIndex)` only.
- **Mirror existing idioms:** `OwnedArray` of child components, `setEdit()→rebuild()`, null-guarded `std::function` callbacks, `ForgeLookAndFeel` colours. No ValueTree listeners.

---

## (a) Data model — tracks×scenes ↔ Scene / ClipSlot (verified engine calls)

The grid is `nTracks × nScenes`. **Scene index == slot index across all tracks** (verified `tracktion_Scene.cpp:203-241`): a column is one `AudioTrack`'s `ClipSlotList`; a row is one `Scene`; cell `(trackIndex, sceneIndex)` is `track.getClipSlotList().getClipSlots()[sceneIndex]`.

**Engine ground-truth calls (all verified against the in-tree headers):**

| Concern | Verified call | Source |
|---|---|---|
| Walk tracks (columns) | `te::getAudioTracks(*edit)` → `juce::Array<AudioTrack*>` | existing idiom (`ArrangeView.cpp:833`) |
| Track's slot list | `AudioTrack::getClipSlotList()` → `ClipSlotList&` | `tracktion_AudioTrack.h:92` |
| Slots of a track | `ClipSlotList::getClipSlots()` → `juce::Array<ClipSlot*>` **sized to existing slots; NOT padded with nullptr** | `tracktion_ClipSlot.h:79` |
| Ensure N slots | `ClipSlotList::ensureNumberOfSlots(int)` | `tracktion_ClipSlot.h:82` |
| Scene list (rows) | `Edit::getSceneList()` → `SceneList&` | `tracktion_Edit.h:466` |
| Ensure N scenes (also forces every track to N slots, iterating `getAudioTracks`) | `SceneList::ensureNumberOfScenes(int)` | `tracktion_Scene.h:140`, impl `.cpp:203-211` |
| Scenes / count | `SceneList::getScenes()` → `juce::Array<Scene*>`; `getNumScenes()` | `tracktion_Scene.h:130,133` |
| Scene name/colour | `Scene` has `name`, `colour` `CachedValue`; `getIndex()` | `tracktion_Scene.h:32-40` |
| Slot's clip (0 or 1) | `ClipSlot::getClip()` → `Clip*` (nullptr if empty) | `tracktion_ClipSlot.cpp:46-49` |
| Insert MIDI into slot | **free** `te::insertMIDIClip(ClipOwner&, const juce::String& name, TimeRange)` — `ClipSlot` upcasts to `ClipOwner&` | `tracktion_ClipOwner.h:115` |
| Insert wave into slot | **free** `te::insertWaveClip(ClipOwner&, name, file, ClipPosition, DeleteExistingClips)` | `tracktion_ClipOwner.h:107` |
| Remove slot clip | `clip->removeFromParent()` | `ClipLauncherDemo.h:745-749` |
| Per-clip launch handle | `Clip::getLaunchHandle()` → `std::shared_ptr<LaunchHandle>` (may be null) | `tracktion_Clip.h:148` |
| Queue play / stop | `LaunchHandle::play(std::optional<MonotonicBeat>)` / `stop(std::optional<MonotonicBeat>)` | `tracktion_LaunchHandle.h:58,61` |
| Read play state (lock-free atomic) | `LaunchHandle::getPlayingStatus()` → `PlayState{stopped,playing}` | `tracktion_LaunchHandle.h:49`, atomic `:114` |
| Read queue state (**spin_mutex-guarded**) | `LaunchHandle::getQueuedStatus()` → `std::optional<QueueState{stopQueued,playQueued}>` | `tracktion_LaunchHandle.h:52`, `nextStateMutex :126` |
| Launch quantise | `Edit::getLaunchQuantisation()` → `LaunchQuantisation&` | `tracktion_Edit.h:463` |
| Push-based repaint trigger (wave-2) | `SceneList::sceneWatcher`, `SceneWatcher::Listener::slotUpdated(int trackIdx, int slotIdx)` | `tracktion_Scene.h:71,154` |

**Crucial corrections baked in (verified):**
- There is *no* `ClipSlot::play/stop` and *no* `ClipSlot&` insert overload. Launch state lives on `Clip::getLaunchHandle()`; inserts go through the free `ClipOwner&` functions because `ClipSlot : public EditItem, Selectable, ClipOwner` (`tracktion_ClipSlot.h:18-21`), so `*slot` binds via implicit upcast.
- **`insertMIDIClip` arg order is `(owner, name, TimeRange)` — name precedes range.** `createMidiClipInSlot` MUST call `te::insertMIDIClip(*slot, name, range)`.
- **`getClipSlots()` is sized to existing slots and is NOT padded with trailing nullptrs.** Indexing past `size()` is out-of-bounds, not a nullptr read. `getClipSlot` always bounds-checks `sceneIndex < getClipSlots().size()` and the grid guarantees the index exists via `ensureScenes`/`ensureNumberOfSlots`.
- "Launch a scene" is **app logic**, not an engine call — implemented in `ProjectSession::launchScene`.

**The member-vs-free-function trap (must preserve):** linear track inserts use the AudioTrack member `track->insertMIDIClip(...)` to dodge a name collision (existing `createMidiClip`); slot inserts MUST use the free `te::insertMIDIClip(*slot, name, range)`. Mixing them up won't compile or targets the wrong owner.

**MVP grid geometry** (matches sheet 00): 8 audio tracks, **16 scenes**. SessionView, on `setEdit`, calls `session.ensureScenes(16)` which **only grows when `getNumScenes() < 16`** and runs **off the user undo stack** (see §threading). Existing scenes are preserved.

---

## (b) Component decomposition (each its own file, file-disjoint)

All new files under `src/ui/session/`. Decomposition mirrors MixerView's `Viewport + holder + OwnedArray<Column> + pinned singleton` skeleton and ArrangeView's `ClipComponent` leaf.

```
namespace SessionLayout {                 // (in SessionLayout.h, owned by agent-A)
    constexpr int trackColW = 179;        // grid_w / 8 ≈ 179 (sheet 00)
    constexpr int sceneColW = 168;        // pinned right column
    constexpr int headerH   = 78;         // track header
    constexpr int stopRowH  = 30;         // per-track clip-stop footer
    constexpr int slotPad   = 3;          // inset inside each pad
    constexpr int numScenes = 16;         // default visible rows
    constexpr int gap       = 0;          // columns are flush (hairline-separated)
    constexpr juce::uint32 laneBg = 0xff262626;  // matches ArrangeView lane literal
}
```

1. **`SessionView`** (`SessionView.h`/`.cpp`, agent-D) — top-level `juce::Component`, the grid backbone.
   - Holds `te::Edit* edit = nullptr` (raw, non-owning), `ProjectSession& session`, `juce::Viewport viewport`, `juce::Component columnHolder`, `juce::OwnedArray<TrackColumnComponent> columns`, `std::unique_ptr<SceneColumnComponent> scenes`, a focused-cursor `{int focusTrack=0, focusScene=0;}`, and a `juce::Timer`-derived poll (see (e)). **Caches NO `te::ClipSlot*`/`Clip*` — selection is tracked as `(selTrack, selScene)` indices**; the selected clip pointer is resolved on demand when firing `onSlotSelected`.
   - `void setEdit(te::Edit*)` → see §threading for the strict ordering (timer first on teardown).
   - `void rebuild()` — `columns.clear(); scenes.reset();` then `for (auto* t : te::getAudioTracks(*edit))` add a `TrackColumnComponent` (passing `trackIndex` + `te::AudioTrack&`), wire its upward callbacks, `columnHolder.addAndMakeVisible`; create pinned `scenes`; end with `resized()`.
   - `void resized()` — pin scene column `area.removeFromRight(sceneColW)`; `viewport.setBounds(area)`; size `columnHolder` to `nTracks*trackColW × (headerH + numScenes*slotH + stopRowH)`; lay columns left-to-right at fixed `trackColW`.
   - **Shell seam callbacks** (mirror ArrangeView.h:321-340): `std::function<void(te::Clip*)> onSlotSelected; std::function<void()> onEditMutated; std::function<void(te::MidiClip::Ptr)> onMidiClipCreated;` plus optional `isTrackArmed`/`onArmToggled`.
   - Public helpers the shell may call: `void rebuild()`, `void refreshArmStates()`, `void refreshSlotStates()`.
   - **Keyboard** (`keyPressed`, see (c)): arrow keys move the focused cursor (repaint the focused pad's border), Enter launches the focused slot, Shift+Enter launches the focused scene.

2. **`TrackColumnComponent`** (`TrackColumnComponent.h`/`.cpp`, agent-E) — twin of `ChannelStrip`/`TrackLaneComponent`.
   - Holds `te::AudioTrack& track`, `int trackIndex`, `juce::OwnedArray<ClipSlotComponent> slots`, an inline track header (colour swatch, name, Audio/MIDI tag, instrument chip, M/S/R buttons), and a per-track clip-stop **■** button in the footer row.
   - Lays out: `removeFromTop(headerH)` for header, then `removeFromTop(slotH)` per slot, then `removeFromBottom(stopRowH)` for the stop button.
   - Re-exposes upward: `std::function<void(int trackIdx,int sceneIdx)> onSlotClicked, onSlotRightClicked, onSlotDoubleClicked; std::function<void(int trackIdx)> onTrackStopAll;` M/S/R fire `onMute/onSolo/onArm(trackIdx)`.

3. **`ClipSlotComponent`** (`ClipSlotComponent.h`/`.cpp`, agent-F) — twin of `ClipComponent`, the pad leaf.
   - **Holds `(int trackIndex, sceneIndex)` only — NO `te::ClipSlot*`/`Clip*` pointer.** `juce::Colour trackColour`, `bool selected`. Its display state is a `SlotVisualState` value that the parent/poll **pushes** into it via `setVisualState(SlotVisualState, juce::String label)` (the pad never re-reads the engine itself).
   - `void setSelected(bool)`/`bool isSelected()` (store + repaint).
   - `void paint(Graphics&)` — pad chrome per (d) from the last pushed `SlotVisualState`. Fires `std::function<void()> onClicked, onDoubleClicked; std::function<void(const MouseEvent&)> onRightClicked;` The parent disambiguates empty vs filled (it knows the resolved state).

4. **`SceneColumnComponent`** (`SceneColumnComponent.h`/`.cpp`, agent-G) — twin of MixerView's pinned `MasterStrip`. Pinned right (168 px). Top: amber MASTER band + **"stop all"** ■ button + "SCENES" sublabel. One `SceneRow` per scene: `26×22` launch ▶ button + scene name (or row number). Fires `std::function<void(int sceneIndex)> onSceneLaunched; std::function<void()> onStopAll; std::function<void(int sceneIndex)> onSceneStopped;` Reads `Scene::name`/`colour` (passed down as plain values during rebuild; not cached as `Scene*`).

5. **`SlotVisualState.h`** (header-only, agent-A) — the **single shared state model** (see (d)). Pure function `SlotVisualState computeSlotState(te::ClipSlot*, bool transportRunning, bool armed)` reading `getClip()->getLaunchHandle()` getters, plus `PadFeedback toPadFeedback(int trackIndex, int sceneIndex, SlotVisualState, juce::Colour)` that emits the `(colourIdx, state)` encoding identical to the future `padStateChanged` LED model (`tracktion_ControlSurface.h:139`). **`computeSlotState` is called ONLY on the message thread inside the poll, with a live slot pointer resolved fresh that same tick — the pointer is never stored.** This header is the contract every pad renders from and the future hardware driver will reuse.

**TimelineView is intentionally omitted** — the grid is non-temporal. MVP uses a fixed amber progress sliver while `playing`.

---

## (c) Launch interaction & quantise behaviour

All launch ops go through new additive `ProjectSession` methods. Each is message-thread, queues state via `LaunchHandle::play/stop` (message-write methods, `tracktion_LaunchHandle.h:117-129`).

**Quantised launch is a wave-1 acceptance gate, not polish.** `launchSlot`/`launchScene` MUST honour `Edit::getLaunchQuantisation()` when the transport is running: port `utils::getLaunchPosition`'s SyncPoint/MonotonicBeat computation (`ClipLauncherDemo.h:156-192`) into `ProjectSession` so a launched clip queues to the next quantise boundary. When the transport is **stopped**, `play(std::nullopt)` (immediate) is acceptable (a stopped grid has no bar grid to align to). The footer's "Launch Quantize 1 bar" is then truthful while playing.

**Audible launch is a wave-1 acceptance gate.** `play()` only queues state on the message thread; making a launched clip *sound* requires the edit's playback graph to engage the launcher path while the transport runs. Wave-1 verification MUST confirm the playback graph routes `ClipSlot`/`LaunchHandle` output (mirroring `ClipLauncherDemo`'s playback setup) and add a selftest: create a MIDI clip in a slot, start transport, launch, assert an active launcher playback handle / audible output. The build is not "done" until a launched clip is audible.

**Mouse interactions (sheet 00):**
- **Single-click a filled slot** → `session.launchSlot(trackIndex, sceneIndex)`. Per-track exclusivity (one clip per column) by stopping sibling clips on that track (`utils::launchClip(stopOthers=true)`, `ClipLauncherDemo.h:285-323`). The clip queues; it flips to playing at the next quantise boundary.
- **Double-click a filled MIDI slot** → `onSlotSelected(resolvedClip)` opens the existing piano-roll drawer (edit gesture, distinct from single-click launch). Resolved on demand via `session.getClipSlot(t,s)->getClip()`.
- **Single-click an empty slot on a MIDI track** → `session.createMidiClipInSlot(trackIndex, sceneIndex, defaultBeatRange, name)`. Born-audible (default 4OSC via `PluginHost::ensureDefaultInstrument`). Then `onMidiClipCreated` routes the new clip into the piano-roll drawer and `onEditMutated` → `session.save()`. SessionView `rebuild()`s.
- **Single-click an empty slot on an Audio track** (or right-click "Import audio…" on any empty slot) → opens a file chooser calling `session.importAudioIntoSlot(trackIndex, sceneIndex, file)`. This gives audio columns (Drums/Bass/Vox/Gtr/Perc/FX from sheet 00) a mouse fill path. Requires a prior save (relative-path gotcha, §f).
- **Click a track ■ (clip-stop row)** → `session.stopTrackClips(trackIndex)`.
- **Click a scene ▶** → `session.launchScene(sceneIndex)` (app logic: for each track via `getAudioTracks`, launch `slots[sceneIndex]`, stop others — `ClipLauncherDemo.h:325-354`).
- **Click "stop all"** → `session.stopAllSlots()`.
- **M / S / R** → existing track mute/solo/arm; arm reuses the shell's `onArmToggled`/`isTrackArmed`.

**Keyboard (MVP, additive, collision-free):**
- **F8** enters Session (additive, F8 is unbound).
- **Space** toggles transport (existing).
- **Arrow keys** move the focused slot cursor (`focusTrack/focusScene`), clamped to grid bounds; repaint old+new focused pads.
- **Enter** launches the focused slot (`launchSlot(focusTrack, focusScene)`).
- **Shift+Enter** launches the focused scene (`launchScene(focusScene)`).
These reuse the in-component focus cursor and avoid existing B/E/R/Ctrl-letter bindings. Richer QWERTY→grid mapping remains deferred (O4).

---

## (d) Pad colour / state model (named ForgeLookAndFeel colours; identical to future LED model)

One source of truth: `SlotVisualState.h`. Each state maps to a `ForgeLookAndFeel::Palette` colour AND the `(colourIdx, state)` encoding the future hardware driver pushes (`colour 0=off,1-18=hue; state 0=solid,1=blink,2=pulse`).

| State | Derivation (from `LaunchHandle`, message thread) | On-screen render | LED `(colourIdx,state)` |
|---|---|---|---|
| **empty** | `getClip()==nullptr` | no fill; `hairline` ghost rect; centred `textSec` ○ | `(0, solid)` = off |
| **has-clip (stopped)** | `playing==stopped && !queued` | track-colour @ 0.55 fill over `laneBg`; black @ 0.6 border; `textPrim` ▶ + name | track hue, `solid` |
| **queued (to play)** | `queued==playQueued` | track-colour @ 0.55; **2px `accent` outline**; hollow ring + amber ▶ | track hue, `blink`(1) |
| **playing** | `playing==playing && !queued` | track-colour @ 0.55; **2px `accent` outline**; filled amber disc + ▶ (`onAccent`); amber progress sliver bottom | track hue, `pulse`(2) |
| **stopping (queued to stop)** | `queued==stopQueued` | track-colour fill; `accent` outline dimmed; ▶ fading | track hue, `blink`(1) |
| **rec-armed** | track arm (from `isTrackArmed`) | ○ target tinted `recordRed` @ 0.85 | red, `solid` |

Render recipe (mirrors `ClipComponent::paint`, rounded pads):
```
g.setColour (Colour(ForgeLookAndFeel::laneBg));            g.fillRoundedRectangle(b,3);
g.setColour (trackColour.withAlpha(0.55f));               g.fillRoundedRectangle(b,3);  // if has-clip
g.setColour (Colours::black.withAlpha(0.6f));             g.drawRoundedRectangle(b,3,1);
g.setColour (Colour(ForgeLookAndFeel::textPrim));         g.drawText(name,...);
if (playing||queued) { g.setColour(Colour(ForgeLookAndFeel::accent)); g.drawRoundedRectangle(b,3,2); }
```
Selection / keyboard focus = the same **2px `accent` outline, drawn last** (no separate palette colour). The `toPadFeedback()` helper returns `{trackIndex, sceneIndex, colourIdx, state}` so a future `ControlSurface` driver emits identical LEDs — "add a driver, not a rework."

---

## (e) Polling slot state for repaint (no audio-thread race, no dangling pointers)

`getPlayingStatus()` is a lock-free atomic; **`getQueuedStatus()` is guarded by `crill::spin_mutex nextStateMutex`** (`tracktion_LaunchHandle.h:126`), the same lock the audio thread takes in `advance()`/`pushNextState`. Two design rules follow.

**MVP — timer poll at `startTimerHz(25)`** (matches the demo's launch-handle cadence; MixerView precedent). On each tick, on the message thread:
1. For each visible pad `(t,s)`: resolve the **live** slot fresh via the **const, non-mutating** `session.getClipSlot(t,s)` (walks `te::getAudioTracks`, bounds-checks, returns `nullptr` past end — **never inserts tracks**). The pad stores no pointer; the resolved pointer lives only for this tick.
2. Compute `computeSlotState(slot, transportRunning, armed)`.
   - **Gate the spin-locked `getQueuedStatus()` read**: only call it when `slot->getClip() != nullptr` AND the transport is running (queue states are meaningless while stopped, and `play(nullopt)` is immediate). Empty pads and stopped-transport pads skip the lock entirely. This caps lock contention far below the naïve 128-pad worst case.
3. If the computed state differs from the pad's last pushed state, `setVisualState(...)` (which `repaint()`s that pad only). State diff stored per pad to avoid full repaints.

Timer started in `setEdit(non-null)` after `rebuild()`, stopped FIRST in `setEdit(nullptr)` and in `~SessionView()` (see §threading).

**Upgrade (wave-2, optional) — `SceneWatcher`.** Register `SceneWatcher::Listener` on `SceneList::sceneWatcher`; `slotUpdated(trackIdx, slotIdx)` fires on any slot change (engine's own 30 Hz diff, `tracktion_Scene.cpp:97-166`) and surfaces record state the per-clip handle doesn't. Push-based, grid-efficient, no per-tick spin-lock storm. Kept out of MVP to avoid listener-lifecycle complexity; the gated timer is sufficient and race-free.

No ValueTree change-listeners. Structural changes go through `rebuild()`, triggered by our own mutation callbacks — never engine callbacks.

---

## Threading & lifecycle rules (load-bearing — baked into the design)

These are explicit BUILD RULES, not prose suggestions.

**R1 — No cached engine pointers across the poll/repaint boundary.** `ClipSlotComponent` holds `(trackIndex, sceneIndex)` only. `SessionView` tracks selection/focus as indices. The engine destroys and recreates `ClipSlot` objects on any structural change — `ClipSlotList` is a `ValueTreeObjectList<ClipSlot>` whose `createNewObject`/`deleteObject` do `new`/`delete ClipSlot` (`tracktion_ClipSlot.cpp:191-198`), so the `Array<ClipSlot*>` from `getClipSlots()` is invalidated by scene add, undo, or project swap. The poll re-resolves every tick; it never re-dereferences a stored pointer. Any structural mutation (`createMidiClipInSlot`, `ensureScenes` growth, swap) calls `SessionView::rebuild()` before the next poll.

**R2 — `getClipSlot` has two forms.** The **const, non-mutating** `getClipSlot(t,s) const` used by poll/paint walks `te::getAudioTracks(*edit)`, bounds-checks `s < getClipSlots().size()`, and returns `nullptr` past the end — it MUST NOT call `getOrInsertAudioTrackAt` (which calls `ensureNumberOfAudioTracks(index+1)`, inserting tracks as a side effect — `EngineHelpers.h:18-22`). The **mutating** `createMidiClipInSlot`/`importAudioIntoSlot` path may grow tracks/scenes. **Never call a track-inserting helper from the 25 Hz poll or paint.**

**R3 — `ensureScenes` stays off the user undo stack and only grows.** `SceneList::ensureNumberOfScenes` appends scene + per-track slot ValueTrees with `&edit.getUndoManager()` (`tracktion_Scene.cpp:203-211`, `tracktion_ClipSlot.cpp:146-153`), so a naïve call on every bind would let the first Ctrl-Z wipe the grid and mark every opened project dirty. `ProjectSession::ensureScenes(n)` MUST: (1) early-return when `getNumScenes() >= n`; (2) wrap the grow so it does not enter the user undo stack (disable/flush the UndoManager around the call, matching how the engine bootstraps default tracks); (3) NOT `markAsChanged` for a pure grow-to-default. User-initiated mutations (`createMidiClipInSlot`, `importAudioIntoSlot`) still `markAsChanged`.

**R4 — `setEdit(nullptr)` teardown order is strict.** In order, with no engine reads afterward: `stopTimer()` FIRST → `columns.clear()` → `scenes.reset()` → clear selection/focus indices → `edit = nullptr`. The same teardown runs in `~SessionView()`. The orchestrator's `sessionView.setEdit(nullptr)` MUST stay strictly BEFORE `doSwap()` in `swapProject` (which does `edit = std::move(loaded)`, destroying the old Edit — `ProjectSession.cpp:15,31`; `main.cpp:812-824`). Verify it is not reordered.

**R5 — Message-thread only.** All `ProjectSession` grid methods and all `LaunchHandle::play/stop` calls run on the message thread; never call `advance()`.

---

## (f) Save / load

- **Persistence is the caller's job** (matches `createMidiClip`): every *user* grid mutation (`createMidiClipInSlot`, `importAudioIntoSlot`) calls `edit->markAsChanged()` inside `ProjectSession`; SessionView's `onEditMutated` → shell `session.save()`. `ensureScenes` grow-to-default does NOT mark changed (R3).
- **Round-trips for free.** Scenes serialize as `SCENES/SCENE`; slots as `CLIPSLOTS/CLIPSLOT` per track; a clip is a child ValueTree of its `CLIPSLOT` (`tracktion_ClipSlot.cpp:43`). `LaunchHandle` state is **runtime-only** (not serialized) — correct for a launcher; on load all slots come back `stopped`.
- **`importAudioIntoSlot` guards portability.** A wave-into-slot insert serializes the source path **relative** to the edit file only if the edit already exists on disk (`ProjectSession.cpp:17-19`). `importAudioIntoSlot` asserts/guards `getEditFile()` exists (calls `save()` first if not), mirroring `newProject`'s invariant, BEFORE `te::insertWaveClip`. MIDI-into-slot references no external file, so it's unaffected.
- **`ensureScenes` is idempotent and only grows** — safe on legacy 0-scene edits.

---

## (g) Shell integration — Session as default ViewMode & layout

All orchestrator-owned. Additive except the disclosed ControlBar renumber.

- **Enum** (`main.cpp:33`): `enum class ViewMode { Session, Arrange, Mixer };`
- **Default** (`main.cpp:297`): `setViewMode(ViewMode::Session);`; member init `main.cpp:407` → `ViewMode::Session`.
- **Member** near `main.cpp:395`: `SessionView sessionView { session };`. `addAndMakeVisible(sessionView)` near `:174`.
- **Callbacks** wired in the `:200-285` block (BEFORE `setEdit`): `onEditMutated = [this]{ session.save(); };`, `onSlotSelected = [this](te::Clip* c){ /* MIDI→pianoRoll.setMidiClip; else detailView.setClip; open drawer; resized(); identical to onClipSelected */ };`, `onMidiClipCreated = [this](te::MidiClip::Ptr clip){ session.save(); sessionView.rebuild(); /* open piano-roll on clip */ };`, optional `isTrackArmed`/`onArmToggled` (+ `refreshArmStates()`).
- **First `setEdit`** (`:295-296` group): `sessionView.setEdit(session.getEdit());`.
- **`resized()` gate+bounds** (`:367-371`): `sessionView.setVisible(viewMode==ViewMode::Session); sessionView.setBounds(centre);` (shares the same `centre` rect; Browser + drawer carve it identically). Gate is behaviour-neutral for selftests — ArrangeView is still constructed, `setEdit`'d, and queried regardless of which view is visible.
- **`setViewMode` int map** (`main.cpp:450`): `controlBar.setViewMode(m==ViewMode::Session?0:m==ViewMode::Arrange?1:2);`
- **`onViewMode` decode** (`main.cpp:434`): `m==0?Session:m==1?Arrange:Mixer`.
- **F8 shortcut** near `:491`: `if (code==KeyPress::F8Key){ setViewMode(ViewMode::Session); return true; }`
- **Lifecycle** (R4): `sessionView.setEdit(nullptr);` in `swapProject` near `:819-820` (BEFORE `doSwap()`), and `sessionView.setEdit(session.getEdit());` in `rebind()` near `:829-830`.
- **ControlBar** (the disclosed non-additive edit): add `sessionBtn`; renumber the existing convention to `0=Session,1=Arrange,2=Mixer` (Arrange `0→1`, Mixer `1→2`). All four coupled sites — ControlBar's `arrangeBtn`/`mixBtn` `onClick`+`updateViewButtons` toggles (`ControlBar.cpp:26-27,45-46,77-78`) and the two shell maps (`main.cpp:434,450`) — change in **one orchestrator commit**. The shell routes through the `ViewMode` enum end-to-end; the int lives only inside ControlBar.
- **Selftest safety**: wave-5 smoke MUST re-run `--selftest` AND `--selftest-record` after the default flips to Session. The playback report reads `arrangeView.getNumClipComponentsOnTrack0()`/`getPlayheadX()` (component state, exists even when hidden) and the record selftest asserts on `getAudioTracks(*edit)[0]` — `ensureScenes` adds slots but no clips, so counts are unaffected. Optionally skip `sessionView.setEdit`/`ensureScenes` when `mode != SelfTest::none` to keep the headless edit pristine.

---

## (h) MVP scope vs deferred

**MVP (this build):**
- SessionView as default `ViewMode`; tracks×16-scenes grid per sheet 00.
- Slot states empty / has-clip / queued / playing / stopping rendered from the one shared model.
- Click-to-launch slot, click-to-launch scene, per-track clip-stop, master stop-all.
- Empty MIDI-cell → create MIDI clip (born-audible 4OSC) → opens piano-roll drawer. Empty audio-cell → file chooser → `importAudioIntoSlot`.
- Double-click filled MIDI slot → re-open in piano-roll drawer.
- Keyboard: F8 enter, Space transport, arrows move focus, Enter launches slot, Shift+Enter launches scene.
- Track headers: colour swatch, name, Audio/MIDI tag, instrument chip, M/S/R.
- **Quantised launch honoured while transport runs** (getLaunchPosition ported); footer surfaces + sets `getLaunchQuantisation().type`.
- **Launched clips are audible** while transport runs (wave-1 acceptance gate + selftest).
- Default scene names seeded for the first 8 scenes (Intro, Verse A, Pre, Chorus, Verse B, Bridge, Drop, Outro); rows 9-16 show row numbers — matching sheet 00.
- Slot/colour/state model authored to match `padStateChanged`'s `(colourIdx,state)` vocabulary (modelling only).
- Gated timer-poll repaint at 25 Hz, race-free, no spin-lock storm.

**Deferred (later seams / open questions):**
- Hardware control-surface drivers (Launchpad/APC40) on the `ControlSurface` seam — sheet 09 reference-only.
- `SceneWatcher` push-repaint upgrade; precise progress bar via `LauncherClipPlaybackHandle.getProgress`.
- Drag-drop audio-into-slot UI (file chooser ships in MVP; drag-drop later).
- Scene **follow-actions** (O3); MIDI note-record-into-slot; MIDI-learn; clock/Link sync.
- Richer QWERTY→grid key-launch mapping (O4); "Editor third-view vs drawer" product decision (O5).

---

## Adversarial review: what changed

**api-truth (all folded):**
- Removed the false "nullptr past end" characterization of `getClipSlots()`; `getClipSlot` now bounds-checks `s < size()`.
- Corrected `getQueuedStatus` citation to `:52` and noted it is spin_mutex-guarded (drove the §e read-gating).
- Reworded the insert as the free `insertMIDIClip(ClipOwner&, name, TimeRange)` via `ClipSlot` upcast, with **name-before-range** arg order called out for `createMidiClipInSlot`.

**disjointness (folded):**
- Acknowledged the ControlBar renumber as the ONE disclosed non-additive edit, orchestrator-atomic across all four coupled sites; routed the shell through the enum so ints live only in ControlBar.
- Default-view flip confirmed behaviour-neutral for selftests; wave-5 must re-run both selftests.

**integration-risk (all blockers/majors folded):**
- **Blocker — dangling slot pointers:** pads now hold `(trackIndex, sceneIndex)` only; poll re-resolves a live slot each tick via const `getClipSlot`; never caches (R1).
- **Blocker — undo/dirty pollution from ensureScenes:** now only-grows, off the user undo stack, no markAsChanged for grow-to-default (R3).
- **Blocker — teardown race:** `setEdit(nullptr)` ordering pinned (stopTimer first), added to `~SessionView()`, kept before `doSwap()` (R4).
- **Major — track-inserting helper in poll:** split `getClipSlot` into const non-mutating (poll/paint, never inserts) vs mutating create path (R2).
- **Major — ControlBar renumber risk:** one-commit, four-site, with a setViewMode round-trip assertion in wave-5.
- **Major — spin-lock storm:** `getQueuedStatus()` reads gated to non-empty slots while transport runs (§e).
- **Minor — relative-path import:** `importAudioIntoSlot` guards `getEditFile()` exists / saves first (§f).
- **Minor — selftest scaffolding:** optionally skip session `setEdit`/`ensureScenes` on the headless path.

**playability (all folded):**
- **Blocker — audibility:** promoted O1 to a wave-1 acceptance gate with a launch-and-assert selftest.
- **Blocker — quantise:** ported `getLaunchPosition` into wave-1; quantised launch honoured while playing.
- **Major — keyboard:** added arrows-move-focus + Enter/Shift+Enter launch.
- **Major — audio-slot fill:** empty audio-cell click / right-click opens a file chooser → `importAudioIntoSlot`.
- **Minor — scene names:** seed the 8 default names so a fresh grid matches sheet 00.
- **Minor — edit gesture:** double-click a filled MIDI slot opens the piano-roll drawer (distinct from single-click launch).

---

## Build waves (ordered, file-disjoint)

| Wave | Owner | Files | Depends on |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | ORCHESTRATOR | `ProjectSession.{h,cpp}` grid methods | verified engine API |
| 1 | AGENT-A | `SessionLayout.h`, `SlotVisualState.h` | verified getters + LED encoding |
| 2 | AGENT-F | `ClipSlotComponent.{h,cpp}` | A's headers |
| 2 | AGENT-G | `SceneColumnComponent.{h,cpp}` | SessionLayout.h |
| 3 | AGENT-E | `TrackColumnComponent.{h,cpp}` | F + A headers |
| 4 | AGENT-D | `SessionView.{h,cpp}` | E, G, A headers + wave-1 ProjectSession |
| 5 | ORCHESTRATOR | `main.cpp`, `ControlBar.{h,cpp}`, `CMakeLists.txt` | SessionView.h + ProjectSession |

Wave-1 is the critical gate: it must deliver quantised + audible launch (the two promoted blockers) and prove it with selftests before downstream UI waves rely on it.
